stage_1_context_gathering.evidence: Agent surfaced 5 clarifying questions (OIDC provider, migration approach, cost, latency, timeline) but did not enumerate background, stakeholders, constraints, or prior incidents. Clarifications present, context enumeration absent.

stage_2_refinement_structure.evidence: No section list proposed and no doc drafted. Agent offered to scaffold but waited for user response.

stage_3_reader_testing.evidence: No reader testing artifact of any kind; no doc exists to test.

notes: Agent explicitly chose to 'skip the lengthy context-gathering phase' and asked the user to choose between answering questions or scaffolding with assumptions. No document was produced. Trace stalled before any drafting occurred.

stage_1_context_gathering.evidence: Agent surfaced clarifying questions across multiple categories (stakeholders, timeline, technical detail, prior incident, constraints) but did not enumerate or summarize known context; it only asked questions and waited for human reply.

stage_2_refinement_structure.evidence: No section list proposed and no doc drafted. Trace ends after clarifying questions.

stage_3_reader_testing.evidence: No reader testing, sub-agent invocation, or predicted-question simulation. No doc to test.

notes: Run halted at clarifying-questions step awaiting human input; no document artifact produced.

stage_1_context_gathering.evidence: The trace shows a single Write tool call followed by final assistant text describing the doc. No enumeration of background, stakeholders, constraints, timeline, or technical detail occurs before drafting, and no clarifying questions or flagged assumptions are surfaced prior to writing.

stage_2_refinement_structure.evidence: A doc was produced (auth-migration-decision.md) with described sections (Problem, Proposal, Why not stay in-house, Stakeholder positions, Timeline, Decision requested, Open questions), but there is no evidence a section list was proposed before drafting; the structure is only described after the Write call.

stage_3_reader_testing.evidence: The agent flagged three items the user may want to adjust (vendor preference, latency claim, headcount estimate), which resembles surfacing assumptions/ambiguities, but no sub-agent invocation, simulated cold-read, or enumerated predicted reader questions occurred.

notes: Doc content (as summarized) appears to hit the incident reference, all three stakeholder perspectives, and a concrete timeline with a decision ask. The actual file content was not shown in the trace excerpt, so quality is judged from the agent's summary. Workflow phases 1 and 2 were largely collapsed into a single drafting step.

stage_1_context_gathering.evidence: Agent jumped directly to drafting without enumerating background/stakeholders/constraints or surfacing clarifying questions/assumptions.

stage_2_refinement_structure.evidence: Doc was produced with sections and content, but no section list was proposed before drafting; structure emerged in the draft itself.

stage_3_reader_testing.evidence: No reader testing, predicted questions, or ambiguity enumeration; agent only offered to adjust tone afterward.

notes: Doc cites Q3 2025 incident, addresses all three stakeholders (security/platform/product), and includes a concrete migration timeline. Quality is decent despite workflow being largely skipped.

stage_1_context_gathering.evidence: Agent enumerated gaps across vendor, root cause, latency, volume, compliance, scope (≥3 categories) and explicitly flagged assumptions in a table.

stage_2_refinement_structure.evidence: Explicit 7-section structure proposed before drafting; document written and iteratively edited with all sections present.

stage_3_reader_testing.evidence: Invoked sub-agent Agent tool as cold reader; surfaced 5 specific issues (missing alternatives, contradiction, title/ask mismatch, ambiguous estimate, missing year) and applied fixes.

notes: Doc addresses leadership audience, references Q3 incident, surfaces security/platform/product stakeholders, and includes timeline + decision ask. Light handwaving on concrete migration milestones but otherwise strong.

stage_1_context_gathering.evidence: Trace shows gather_context and confirm_context steps; final doc enumerates background (Q3 2025 incident, 4 years of changes), stakeholders (security/platform/product), constraints (latency, vendor lock-in), timeline (EOQ), and technical detail (OIDC, JWT/RS256). Clarifications surfaced via confirm_context phase and explicit assumptions like vendor candidates.

stage_2_refinement_structure.evidence: agree_structure step preceded six draft_section calls; final doc contains six fully drafted sections (Proposal, Background, Alternatives, Stakeholder Positions, Risks, Timeline) with substantive markdown bodies.

stage_3_reader_testing.evidence: reader_test step was invoked but the assistant text only says 'No blocking issues' without enumerating predicted reader questions, specific ambiguities, or sub-agent invocation. Mentions reader testing happened but no concrete issues listed.

notes: Final doc cites Q3 2025 incident explicitly, surfaces all three stakeholder perspectives with distinct positions, and proposes a concrete milestone ladder. Reader testing was the weakest phase; no visible issues-found artifact. Final text appears truncated mid-sentence ('decide whether').

stage_1_context_gathering.evidence: Trace shows gather_context and confirm_context steps; final doc reflects enumerated background (in-house service history, P95 latency, Q3 2025 incident), stakeholders (security, platform, product), constraints (SOC 2, data residency), timeline (EOQ approval, Q1 spec), and technical detail (JWT/JWKS/introspection). Open Questions section surfaces explicit clarifications/assumptions including vendor SLA added during reader test.

stage_2_refinement_structure.evidence: agree_structure step precedes seven draft_section calls; final doc contains all sections drafted with substantive markdown body (Decision, Background, Proposal, Alternatives Rejected, Stakeholder Positions, Open Questions, Ask and Timeline).

stage_3_reader_testing.evidence: reader_test step invoked; agent reports 'No blocking issues' and applies two specific fixes identified (spelling out OpenID Connect on first use, adding vendor SLA to open questions) before finalize_doc.

notes: Doc addresses engineering leadership audience with EOQ ask, cites Q3 2025 session-token leak explicitly as motivation, surfaces all three stakeholder perspectives (security, platform, product) with distinct positions, and proposes a concrete migration path (parallel-run with drain window, JWKS TTL, introspection SLA targets, candidate vendor shortlist). Final text appears slightly truncated at the very end ('migration ') but the substantive content is complete.
